name: pyright
description: Microsoft's static type checker and language server for Python — provides code completion, go-to-definition, type inference, diagnostics, and refactoring support
when_to_use: Always active for Python projects — provides IDE intelligence, catches type errors before runtime

name: pip install
description: Python's default package installer — downloads and installs packages from PyPI and other indexes
when_to_use: To install project dependencies, add new packages, or set up a Python environment

name: uv
description: Extremely fast Python package manager and resolver written in Rust — drop-in replacement for pip, pip-tools, and virtualenv with 10-100x speedup
when_to_use: As a faster alternative to pip for installing packages, resolving dependencies, managing virtualenvs, and running Python scripts

name: poetry
description: Python dependency management and packaging tool — manages pyproject.toml, lockfiles, virtualenvs, and publishing to PyPI in one workflow
when_to_use: For projects using pyproject.toml with poetry — manages dependencies, lockfile resolution, virtualenvs, and package builds

name: pytest
description: Python's most popular test framework — supports fixtures, parametrize, markers, plugins, and rich assertion introspection with minimal boilerplate
when_to_use: After code changes, before commits, in CI pipelines — the standard way to run Python tests
what_it_finds: Test failures, assertion errors, exceptions, fixture issues

name: pytest-cov
description: Pytest plugin for measuring code coverage — wraps coverage.py to report which lines of code are exercised by tests
when_to_use: To measure test coverage, find untested code paths, enforce coverage thresholds in CI
what_it_finds: Line and branch coverage percentages, uncovered code paths, coverage regressions

name: pytest-xdist
description: Pytest plugin for parallel test execution — distributes tests across multiple CPUs or remote machines to speed up test suites
when_to_use: When test suites are slow and can benefit from parallel execution across CPU cores
what_it_finds: Same as pytest but runs tests in parallel for faster feedback

name: ruff
description: Extremely fast Python linter and formatter written in Rust — replaces flake8, black, isort, pydocstyle, pyupgrade, and dozens of other tools with a single binary that is 10-100x faster
when_to_use: Before every commit for linting and formatting — the modern all-in-one Python code quality tool
what_it_finds: Style violations, unused imports, type annotation issues, security problems, complexity, import ordering

name: mypy
description: The original static type checker for Python — validates type annotations, catches type errors before runtime, and enforces type safety across the codebase
when_to_use: Before commits, in CI — catches type mismatches, missing return types, incorrect function signatures
what_it_finds: Type errors, incompatible types, missing annotations, unreachable code, protocol violations

name: black
description: The uncompromising Python code formatter — deterministically formats code to a single canonical style, eliminating all style debates
when_to_use: Before every commit to ensure consistent code style — or use ruff format as a faster alternative

name: pylint
description: Comprehensive Python linter that checks for errors, enforces coding standards, detects code smells, and provides refactoring suggestions with a scoring system
when_to_use: For deep static analysis beyond what ruff provides — catches design issues, naming conventions, and complex code smells
what_it_finds: Code smells, design problems, naming violations, unreachable code, duplicate code, missing docstrings

name: bandit
description: Security-focused static analysis tool for Python — scans code for common security issues like SQL injection, hardcoded passwords, insecure deserialization, and shell injection
when_to_use: Before releases, in CI, during security audits — catches common Python security anti-patterns
what_it_finds: SQL injection, hardcoded secrets, insecure random, shell injection, insecure deserialization, weak cryptography

name: safety
description: Checks installed Python packages against a database of known security vulnerabilities — scans requirements files or the current environment for CVEs
when_to_use: Before releases, in CI, after dependency updates — ensures no known-vulnerable packages are installed
what_it_finds: CVEs in installed packages, vulnerable dependency versions, outdated packages with known exploits

name: pip-audit
description: Audits Python environments and requirements files for packages with known vulnerabilities using the OSV and PyPI vulnerability databases
when_to_use: Before releases, in CI — an alternative to safety backed by Google's OSV database for broader vulnerability coverage
what_it_finds: CVEs and known vulnerabilities in Python dependencies, available fix versions

name: tox
description: Test automation tool that runs tests in isolated virtualenvs across multiple Python versions — standard for verifying cross-version compatibility
when_to_use: To test against multiple Python versions, automate test matrix execution, in CI pipelines for library compatibility testing

name: nox
description: Modern test automation tool using Python scripts instead of INI config — a flexible alternative to tox with sessions defined as Python functions in noxfile.py
when_to_use: For test automation when you want Python-scriptable sessions instead of tox's INI-based config — popular with Google and major open-source projects

name: pre-commit
description: Framework for managing and maintaining multi-language git hook scripts — automatically runs linters, formatters, and checks before each commit
when_to_use: To enforce code quality checks (linting, formatting, type checking) automatically before every git commit

name: pdb
description: Python's built-in interactive debugger — set breakpoints, step through code, inspect variables, and evaluate expressions at runtime
when_to_use: When debugging complex issues that require stepping through code execution, inspecting state, or understanding control flow
what_it_finds: Runtime bugs, incorrect variable state, unexpected control flow, exception sources

name: py-spy
description: Sampling profiler for Python programs written in Rust — attaches to running processes without restarting or modifying code, produces flame graphs and top-like live views
when_to_use: To profile running Python processes, find CPU bottlenecks, generate flame graphs without code changes or restarts
what_it_finds: CPU hotspots, slow functions, performance bottlenecks, GIL contention

name: memray
description: Memory profiler for Python by Bloomberg — tracks every memory allocation in Python and native C/C++ extensions with minimal overhead, produces flame graphs and reports
when_to_use: To find memory leaks, understand memory usage patterns, identify allocation hotspots in Python applications
what_it_finds: Memory leaks, high-allocation functions, native extension memory usage, allocation patterns over time

name: django check
description: Django's built-in system check framework — inspects models, settings, URLs, templates, and security configuration for common issues and misconfigurations
when_to_use: After Django model changes, before deployments, to validate project configuration and catch common Django mistakes
what_it_finds: Model errors, security misconfigurations, deprecated settings, URL routing issues, template problems

name: flask run
description: Flask's built-in development server with hot reloading — starts a local server for development and testing of Flask web applications
when_to_use: During local development of Flask applications — provides auto-reload on code changes and debug mode with interactive traceback

name: fastapi dev
description: FastAPI's development server using uvicorn with auto-reload — starts a local ASGI server for developing and testing FastAPI applications with automatic OpenAPI docs
when_to_use: During local development of FastAPI applications — provides hot reload, interactive API docs at /docs, and automatic request validation
